mappings.rationaleAI: Human anatomy covers human integument (A.7), human skeletal system (B.7), vertebral column (B.4.c), appendicular skeleton (B.4.d), joints (B.5), bone properties (B.6), and vertebrate muscles (C.5).
Physiology covers skin biodynamics (A.6), muscle tissue features (C.1), muscle contraction (C.3), and muscle regulation (C.5.c).
Animal diversity covers body covering comparisons (A.1), invertebrate integuments (A.2), invertebrate skeleton (B.3), and invertebrate muscles (C.4).
Development covers skin embryology (A.5), skeletal embryology (B.4.b), and muscular embryonic development (C.5.a).
Teeth cover cuticular structures (B.2.a), vertebrate skeleton (B.4), and bone development (B.6).
Mammals cover vertebrate integuments including hair (A.3, A.3.b), vertebrate skeleton (B.4, B.4.a), and muscle systems (C.5).
Reptiles cover vertebrate integuments including scales (A.3, A.3.b), skin variations (A.4), and vertebrate skeleton (B.4).
Insects cover invertebrate integuments (A.2), cellular components (A.2.a), noncellular coatings/cuticle (A.2.b), and invertebrate skeleton (B.3).
Ornamental traits connect to skin derivatives including feathers, hair, and pigment (A.3.b).
Evolution explains diversity through skin embryology (A.5), skeletal embryology (B.4.b), and musculatory evolution (C.5.b).
Cell biology covers integument cellular components (A.2.a), contractile systems (C.2, C.2.a), and striated/smooth muscle (C.2.b).
Hormones connect to skin biodynamics (A.6) and muscle regulation (C.5.c).
